options.content:
# **🚀 kube-burner-ocp virt capacity benchmark dashboard**

This dashboard provides deep insights into the results of the virt-capacity-benchmark execution using kube-burner-ocp. For a deep dive into workload parameters and metric definitions, refer to the [official kube-burner-ocp documentation](https://kube-burner.github.io/kube-burner-ocp/latest/#virt-capacity-benchmark).

### ⚙️ How to configure your view

Use the dropdown selectors at the top of the dashboard to filter the results:

- **Run UUID 🔽:** Select the unique identifier for your benchmark execution to filter all visualizations for that specific test run.
- **Latency Percentile 📊:** Toggle between median (50th) for typical performance or tail latencies (90th/95th/99th) to identify worst-case bottlenecks in your infrastructure.

📌 **NOTE:**
- All latency metrics in this dashboard are reported in seconds.
- Since the OS image is provisioned through persistent storage instead of being pulled directly into the `virt-launcher` pod’s local directory, this scenario results in the creation and binding of DataVolumes and PVCs.

targets.query: jobName.keyword:create-vms-* AND metricName.keyword:"dvLatencyMeasurement" AND uuid:"$uuid" AND dvName.keyword:*-root
jobName.keyword:create-vms-* AND metricName.keyword:"dvLatencyMeasurement" AND uuid:"$uuid" AND dvName.keyword:*-data-*
jobName.keyword:create-vms-* AND metricName.keyword:"dvLatencyMeasurement" AND uuid:"$uuid" AND dvName.keyword:*-root
jobName.keyword:create-vms-* AND metricName.keyword:"dvLatencyMeasurement" AND uuid:"$uuid" AND dvName.keyword:*-data-*
jobName.keyword:create-vms-* AND metricName.keyword:"dvLatencyMeasurement" AND dvName.keyword:*-root AND uuid:"$uuid"
jobName.keyword:create-vms-* AND metricName.keyword:"dvLatencyMeasurement" AND uuid:"$uuid" AND dvName.keyword:*-root

list.description: Filters the dashboard to show performance metrics for the selected virt-capacity benchmark run ID
list.label: Run UUID
list.name: uuid

list.description: Selects the P-value (50th to 99th) for VM and DV latency calculations
list.label: Latency Percentile
list.name: percentile
